source_pr.title: dumpling: fix TestSpeedRecorder flaky test
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/39255

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

increased error tolerance

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: TestSpeedRecorder intermittently fails due to timing/scheduling variance when asserting speed calculations after time.Sleep().
failure_signature: require.FailNow("speed is unexpected", "expected: ... recent: ...") in TestSpeedRecorder.
root_cause_explanation: TestSpeedRecorder computes recent speed based on wall-clock timing and uses time.Sleep() in the test loop. The original assertion used an absolute error threshold (abs(expected-recentSpeed) > 1), which is too strict under variable scheduling/GC/CPU load and makes the test flaky. This PR stabilizes it by switching to a relative error threshold (10%).
fix_pattern: Use relative error tolerance when asserting speed recorder output in timing-dependent test.

evidence: evidence: dumpling: fix TestSpeedRecorder flaky test
